title: scapha subcordata
side_effects: Scapha subcordata can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and increased heart rate. Ingesting the plant's extract can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils may cause respiratory problems, such as coughing and wheezing. Improper use of the plant can also interact with certain medications, exacerbating conditions like hypertension and diabetes.
